items.passage: Sleep is far more active than it appears to a casual observer. During the night the brain cycles repeatedly through lighter and deeper stages, each serving a distinct purpose for the body. In the deepest phase, tissues are repaired and the immune system is strengthened against future infection. Later stages, when vivid dreams occur, appear to help the mind sort and store the memories formed during the day. Researchers now believe that consistent, unbroken rest is essential for both clear thinking and lasting emotional balance.

The annual flooding of the Nile shaped almost every aspect of life in ancient Egypt. Each summer the river overflowed its banks and spread a thick layer of dark, fertile silt across the surrounding fields. Farmers depended on this rich deposit to grow the wheat and barley that fed the entire kingdom. Officials carefully measured the height of the rising water to predict whether the harvest would be generous or poor. Because the river proved so reliable, the Egyptians organized their calendar and their festivals around its steady rhythm.

Cosmic radiation consists of tiny, high-energy particles that travel across space at nearly the speed of light. Many of these particles are born in violent stellar explosions far beyond our own solar system. When they strike the upper atmosphere, they collide with gas molecules and produce a shower of lighter fragments. The planet's magnetic field deflects much of this radiation and protects the fragile life that thrives on the surface below. Scientists monitor these particles closely because sudden bursts can disturb satellites and interfere with sensitive electronic equipment.
